content.text: The arithmetic is decisive. Now I'll write the design.

input.thesis: The gap between us and the state of the art is not a knowledge gap, it is an INSTRUMENT gap: the judge already writes a 25-criterion, two-article, raw-score ledger explaining exactly why we lose, and our harness throws it away at deepresearch_bench_race.py:187 — while both plans spend their word count arguing about numbers (cellcog's task-72 score, the value of attribution, the value of recency, whether the judge detects fabrication) that cost about ten dollars and two days to MEASURE. So: build the instrument first, then ABLATE THE CHAMPION — mechanically degrade cellcog's own 16,334-word task-72 artifact one feature at a time and score k=5 — which prices every lever Sol and Fable want to build, in the judge's own currency, at the score level we are targeting, before we write a line of retrieval code.
input.design:
## PART 0 — WHAT I VERIFIED (everything below is load-bearing and was checked against source, not quoted from a brief)

**V1. The score is a RATIO against a document we hold.** `third_party/deep_research_bench/deepresearch_bench_race.py:160`: `overall_score = target_total / (target_total + reference_total)`. The reference is a FIXED 9,029-word Gemini-DR article at `data/test_data/cleaned_data/reference.jsonl` (100 tasks, all present). 0.5 = parity with it. Our 0.4396 means the judge gives us ~0.78x the reference's weighted points; cellcog's 0.5578 is ~1.26x. That is where Sol's "1.62x" comes from — I reproduced it (1.61x) — but neither plan says the target is a *specific 9k-word document sitting on our disk that we can read*.

**V2. The judge's full ledger is computed and DISCARDED.** `deepresearch_bench_race.py:123` builds `llm_output_json`, which contains, for all 25 criteria: our raw 0-10, **the reference's raw 0-10**, and the judge's written comparative analysis of the two. `final_result` at `:187` keeps only 4 normalized dims + overall. We delete the single most valuable text in the project — the grader telling us, in its own words, why we lost, criterion by criterion.

**V3. The board is drop-in scoreable and the missing number is $0.55 away.** Every board jsonl at `/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/*.jsonl` has exactly `{id, prompt, article}` — the harness's raw_data format — and **100/100 exact prompt-string matches** against `query.jsonl` (verified). cellcog's task-72 score, the number both plans argue about for pages, does not exist only because nobody ran `scripts/score_report_race.py` on a file we have had all along.

**V4. Judge cost.** One task-72 call = ~57k input tokens (ref 69k chars + article 124k chars + criteria 11k chars) + ~4k out ≈ **$0.11**. k=5 on one artifact = **$0.55**. All 10 board systems at k=5 = **$5.55**. An 8-task panel at k=3 = **$2.60**. Scoring is free. **Composing is the only real cost.** Both plans budget as if the opposite were true.

**V5. Our own noise data, re-derived.** `results/race/noise_r10_1..5` = five re-scores of the SAME rank10 artifact: 0.4397/0.4330/0.4334/0.4405/0.4512 → **mean 0.4396, SD 0.0074**. That is the provenance of "SD 0.0074". But `noise_ctrl_1..5` gives SD **0.0021** — the judge's noise is heteroscedastic, so 0.0074 is the conservative figure and I use it. Minor correction: the headline **0.4382 is not reproducible** from `results/race`; the stored single run is 0.4313 and the k=5 mean is 0.4396.

**V6. THE GENERALITY NUMBER ALREADY EXISTS AND NOBODY QUOTED IT.** `results/race/polaris_vm_t72/75/76/78/90` = our system run END-TO-END on five tasks (real VM sweep, 851 sources, $5.69, per `WHEEL_PROGRESS.md:413`): **0.2530 / 0.2839 / 0.2646 / 0.2107 / 0.3026, mean 0.263.** On task 72 the *system* scores 0.2530; the hand-iterated *artifact* scores 0.4396. **The 0.19 between them is hand-tuning — larger than the 0.116 gap to SOTA.** `WHEEL_PROGRESS.md:411` already says "2ND-DRB-TASK GENERALITY GATE — BLOCKED."

**V7. The rubric is generated per task, and the weights are NOT constants.** `data/criteria_data/criteria.jsonl` holds all 100 tasks with the judge's own `dimension_weight` + 21–31 weighted criteria, produced by `prompt/criteria_prompt_en.py` from the task text alone. Across the 100 tasks: insight **0.11–0.42**, instruction_following **0.13–0.35**, comprehensiveness 0.20–0.37, readability 0.10–0.25. "INSIGHT is 0.32, chase insight" is a task-72 fact, not a system fact.

**V8. THE ARITHMETIC THAT KILLS THE RETRIEVAL-FIRST PLAN.** Ranking task 72's 25 criteria by total weight (dim_w × crit_w): the two criteria the *entire* journal-corpus apparatus serves — "Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized" (0.0435) and "Exclusive Citation of High-Quality Journal Articles" (0.0375) — total **8.1% of the score**. Driving BOTH from ~5.5 to a near-perfect 9.5 raw buys **+0.0142 overall**. The gap is 0.116. Meanwhile the top two INSIGHT criteria ("Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation", "Analytical Depth in Characterizing Mechanisms") total **16.0%** — the same swing there is worth +0.0273, 1.9x the entire corpus program.
And the deeper consequence: **the score is not additive over levers.** To go 0.4396 → 0.556 you need **+3.34 raw points on the weighted average of all 25 criteria** — a whole-document jump from the 4-6 band ("basically meets") to the 8-10 band ("excellent/outstanding"). *No subset of criteria can close it.* Any plan that lists five levers and sums their deltas is mis-modelling the instrument. The only things that move all 25 criteria at once are **evidenced length × density** and **overall scholarly quality of the prose** — which is precisely what cellcog is (16,334 words, ~100w paragraphs, 173 attributions, 8 named syntheses). Sol's X3 ("rubric bleed") is not a tail risk; by this arithmetic it is the *main term*, and Sol books it at 20%.

---

## PART 1 — THE INSTRUMENT (half a day, ~$10, zero new science)

**P1 — SAVE THE LEDGER.** Patch `deepresearch_bench_race.py:187`, ~6 lines: add `"criteria_scores": llm_output_json` plus `"target_total"/"reference_total"` from `scores` (built at `:153-157`). Every score run now emits:
- **THE DEFICIT LEDGER**: per criterion, `(ref_raw − our_raw) × dim_w × crit_w` = the exact overall-points on the table. Sol spends each turn on the biggest *dimension* (4 buckets); this is 25x finer and costs nothing.
- **R, the reference's raw weighted total** — the denominator of everything. Every number in V8 above assumes R≈7.0; P1 makes it exact.
- **The judge's verbatim comparative analysis** per criterion. This replaces both plans' "READ the prose against the rubric by hand" step. The grader already did that read.

**P2 — THE CALIBRATION RUN (~$5.55).** Copy all 10 board jsonls into `data/test_data/raw_data/`, score task 72, k=5 each.
- 9 have published task-72 scores. Regress ours on theirs. **If slope≈1, offset≈0, residual<0.02, our gpt-5.5 judge IS the leaderboard's judge and every target number in both plans is valid.** Nobody has checked this. Every plan in this project assumes it.
- The 10th is **cellcog**, whose task-72 score does not exist. We read it directly, SE≈0.003. This resolves the single most-argued number in both documents (Sol's 0.5670 gate vs Fable's 0.5578 vs the regression's 0.556±0.019) **with data instead of rhetoric.** It may well come back BELOW bodhi's 0.5441 — in which case both plans have been chasing a phantom.
- Via P1 it also yields cellcog's and bodhi's **per-criterion profiles**. The target stops being a scalar and becomes a vector we can subtract from.
- Free bonus already visible: gemini-DR-as-target scores **0.5102** against a *different* gemini-DR sample as reference — two draws from the same system land 0.010 above parity. That is an upper bound on (slot-1 position bias + within-system variance) worth 1.4 judge-SDs, i.e. ~1/12 of the whole gap, and it is FREE if real. **Swap test**: re-score our artifact in slot 2 (5 calls) and measure it.

---

## PART 2 — THE CHEAPEST EXPERIMENT: ABLATE THE CHAMPION, DON'T BUILD THE CHALLENGER

**The inversion.** Every attempt so far measured feature value by ADDING a lever to our 0.44 artifact — where the lever is confounded with our execution quality, and where the gradient at 0.44 need not be the gradient at 0.55. Five commits (`225b323`, `b3acb72`, `fb228dd`, `e87f52e`, `947d2b5`) are the graveyard of that method.

**We hold cellcog's task-72 artifact (16,334 words). Degrade it one feature at a time and watch what it costs the #1 system.** Execution quality is held at cellcog's level in every arm, so Δ is the feature. The mechanism *is* the treatment — this is not a proxy.

Arms, each k=5 (~$0.55), each a mechanical transform of `cellcog-max.jsonl` id=72:

| arm | transform | what it prices | whose plan it tests |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A0** | unmodified | control + **cellcog's true task-72 score** | the gate itself |
| **A1** | **STRIP ATTRIBUTION** — "Guarascio, Piccirillo, and Reljic (2025), in the *Journal of Economic Surveys*, find…" → "One study finds…". Every claim, number, structure, synthesis, word-count preserved | authorship+journal+year visibility in prose | **Sol's Format D, Fable's Format E, the ≥110-attribution target, Gate 3 cleaner-survival, and the entire judge-facing payoff of the corpus program.** If A1≈A0, three weeks of retrieval buys nothing |
| **A2** | **STRIP SYNTHESIS** — delete §5 (the eight named syntheses), the adjudication sentences, the epigrammatic paragraph-closes; keep all evidence prose | the insight engine | Fable's central bet + the 16%-weight insight pair |
| **A3** | **STRIP RECENCY** — rewrite 2024–2026 attributions to pre-2023 | cellcog's 164 recency mentions / 41-of-104 2024+ refs | **Sol's #1 named risk (35%, "forward citation expansion stays dead") and Fable's entire COCI program.** OPERATOR FIX 1 already retired the *mechanism*; A3 asks whether the *goal* was worth anything |
| **A4** | **STRIP STRUCTURE** — merge to ~600w paragraphs, delete H3/H4, delete the table, delete the structured abstract | readability mechanics | Sol prices this +0.021, Fable +0.023 — both from thin air |
| **A5** | **TRUNCATE TO 8k** — cut to ~8,000 words preserving section proportions and attribution density | SCALE per se | Fable: "any plan capping body length near 8k is fighting #1 with half its ammunition." Per V8, this is the one lever that moves all 25 criteria at once — I expect it to be the largest Δ |
| **A6** | **FABRICATION PROBE** (quarantined instrument, NEVER shipped, never touches the compose path) — swap ~20 real attributions for plausible invented author+journal+number | **can the judge detect fabrication at all?** | Both plans argue faithfulness at length. Nobody has measured its price |

**PRE-REGISTERED READ.** Gap to close = 0.556 − 0.4396 = **0.116**. Δ_i = A0 − A_i, k=5, paired SE ≈ 0.0047 (SD 0.0074 × √(2/5)), min resolvable **0.0092**.
→ **If Σ(Δ_A1..A5) < 0.06** — less than half the gap — the features both plans are *built out of* account for less than half of what separates us from #1, the "visible scholarship pays" thesis is **REFUTED**, and the residue is overall prose/impression quality. We then move the entire budget from the corpus pipeline to composition. **This is the kill artifact: the smallest thing that can tell us the whole plan is wrong. ~$4. One day. Zero retrieval, zero corpus, zero composer.**

**Confound control** (arms A1–A5 must remove the feature and nothing else): length-neutral rewrites where possible; a **mechanism counter per arm**, measured POST-CLEANER (`prompt/clean_prompt.py`) — e.g. A1 must show surviving in-prose author names go 173→~0 while word count moves <3%. If the counter didn't move, the arm didn't happen and its score is inadmissible. P1's per-dimension raw scores let us *see* contamination (if A1 moves readability, the rewrite was not clean).

**Cross-check against baseline interaction** (the honest weakness): a feature worth +0.04 to a 16k-word masterpiece may be worth 0 to our 2k-word draft. So we also run the top-ranked lever as an ADD to our own artifact and require the sign to agree. Two extra arms, ~$1.10.

---

## PART 3 — GENERALITY: THE PANEL IS THE SCOREBOARD, NOT TASK 72

**The measured truth (V6): our system is worth 0.263, not 0.44.** Any plan scored only on task 72 will re-tune the artifact and ship a 0.26 system. So:

**THE PANEL — 8 EN tasks, fixed BEFORE any building, stratified by SHAPE not topic** (from `data/prompt_data/query.jsonl`):
- **t72** literature review, journal-only, academic
- **t75 / t78** clinical-biomedical (metal-ion modulation; Parkinson's warning signs)
- **t90** legal/liability (ADAS accident allocation)
- **t70** software history (Java Servlets → Spring Boot) — **no journals exist**
- **t66** Obsidian plugins replicating Notion databases — **no academic literature exists at all**
- **t93** market research (video-editing software market) — commercial/industry sources
- **t95** Diamond Sutra study notes — textual/religious scholarship

**On 7 of those 8, a journal-corpus builder with DOI cards, Crossref `reference[]` arrays, COCI forward edges and an ISSN allowlist retrieves NOTHING.** Sol's 15 hand-named econ anchors and Fable's citation-percentile weighting are not general mechanisms; they are task-72 furniture. This panel makes that visible on turn 0 instead of at the end.

Headline metric = **panel mean**, reported as a vector plus mean, every turn. Task 72 is one cell of eight. Cost: 8 × k=3 = **$2.60** per full panel score.

**THE FREE, ZERO-JUDGE GENERALITY PROOF.** OPERATOR FIX 4's keystone is real (V7) and it comes with an answer key nobody noticed: run our rubric generator over all 100 prompts and compare to the cached `criteria.jsonl` — weight MAE, and criterion-set overlap by embedding match. **If our generated rubric reconstructs the judge's own rubric on 100/100 tasks, the "derive the plan from the prompt" mechanism is PROVEN GENERAL, offline, for 100 cheap LLM calls and zero judge calls.** It is the cheapest generality proof available and it grades itself against the benchmark's own key. (Honest caveat: this is a *mechanism* test — a perfect rubric with a bad report is still a bad report.)

**THE DERIVED PLAN — every hardcoded constant replaced.** Nothing in this chain names a domain:
1. **RUBRIC** ← the judge's own generator, run on the prompt → weights + N criteria.
2. **SCOPE GATE** (FIX 3) ← derived from the prompt: topic, recency window, **source class**, language. A hard admission gate, not a weight. Task 72 *derives* "peer-reviewed English journal articles" because the prompt says so. Task 66 derives "plugin docs / forum threads / GitHub" and would **reject a journal article as out-of-scope**.
3. **COVERAGE MATRIX** ← rows are **the generated criteria themselves** (they already ARE the coverage spec: "Breadth of Restructuring Dimensions Covered" literally enumerates creation/displacement/transformation/skills/wages/productivity); columns are the entity axis **extracted from the criterion text** ("across a diverse range of relevant industries" → industries). No engineer types an 8×10 grid.
4. **ANCHORS** ← first search → highest-authority on-topic works *within the derived source class* → seeds for expansion. Citation count is only *one realization* of authority (it is meaningless for Obsidian plugins; there, authority = plugin install count / maintainer reputation / forum consensus).
5. **OUTLINE** ← the matrix. **STOPPING** ← matrix saturation.
6. **EFFORT ALLOCATION** ← the derived weights. On a task with insight 0.11 / readability 0.25, the system must behave completely differently, and only a derived allocator will.

**Scope honesty:** 50 of the 100 tasks are Chinese. cellcog's 0.5578 is a 100-task mean including all 50. **We are not going to beat it corpus-wide without composing in Chinese, and I am not proposing that.** Our claim is per-task and EN-panel. Saying otherwise is a units error of exactly the kind Fable already caught once.

---

## PART 4 — THE WHEEL, WITH PRE-REGISTERED KILL RULES

**FIX → COMPOSE → MECHANISM GATE → SCORE k=5 → READ THE JUDGE'S LEDGER → FIX.**

**The ordering breaks with both plans.** Do not run Sol's lever order or Fable's. **Run the levers in the order the Part-2 ablation table ranks them by measured value ÷ build cost.** Both plans assert an order from arithmetic they invented. The ablation hands us the order from data, on day 2.

**GATES:**
- **G0 MECHANISM GATE** (the lesson of `225b323`/`b3acb72`/`fb228dd`): every lever ships with a counter measured on the composed artifact **post-cleaner**. If the counter didn't move, the lever did not happen and **the score run is inadmissible as evidence about it** — before we ask whether the score moved. This is Sol's Gate 3 generalized from journal-name survival to *every* lever.
- **G1 CONTRACT GATE** (FIX 7): `validate()` ON the critical path — it is imported at `cellcog_composer.py:49` and **called nowhere**; `_clean()` at `:342` is the only thing between LLM prose and the page. Mechanism fields get their own verbatim spans (`cellcog_composer.py:167` copies them unchecked today). Poisoned-card CI test. ATTACK#1 stays REJECTED. **Red gate = no scoring money spent.**
- **G2**: the hardcoded abstract at `cellcog_composer.py:400-414` dies — it asserts a compliance claim ("draws exclusively on peer-reviewed… articles identified through citation-graph expansion") regardless of the corpus. That is fabrication with a score motive.

**KILL RULES, in firing order:**
- **K0 — INSTRUMENT KILL (day 1).** If P2's re-score does not reproduce the 9 published task-72 scores (slope≈1, offset≈0, residual <0.02), our judge is not the leaderboard's judge, **every target number in both plans is on a different scale**, and we STOP and recalibrate before building anything.
- **K1 — THESIS KILL (day 2).** If Σ(Δ_A1..A5) < 0.06, the visible-scholarship thesis is refuted. **Do not build the retrieval program.** Reallocate to prose and composition. *This is the answer to "what is the smallest artifact that would tell us the thesis is wrong."*
- **K2 — LEVER KILL.** Keep iff paired Δ ≥ **+0.0092** (1.96 × 0.0074 × √(2/5)) **AND** its G0 mechanism counter moved. Fails either → REVERTED, not tweaked.
- **K3 — OVERFIT KILL (exists in neither plan, and it is the one that saves us).** A lever is kept only if its **panel-mean** delta is non-negative. **A lever that gains on task 72 and loses on the panel is an overfit and is reverted.** Without K3 we ship the 0.26 system.
- **K4 — RUN KILL.** Two consecutive turns gaining < +0.0092 on the **panel mean** → STOP, report MISSED, name the deficit criteria straight from the ledger.
- **K5 — INTEGRITY KILL.** One fabricated number, entity, or attribution on a **shipped** page burns the artifact regardless of score.

**THE STOPPING CONDITION IS DERIVED, NOT ARGUED.** I refuse to choose between Sol's 0.5670 and Fable's 0.5578 **because that choice is currently a debate and it should be a measurement.** After P2 we KNOW cellcog's task-72 score to SE≈0.003. The gate is then: **task-72 k=5 mean ≥ (measured cellcog task-72) + 0.0092, AND panel mean not regressed, AND every integrity gate green.** If the measurement says 0.572, the gate is 0.581. If it says 0.548, the gate is 0.557. Like every other constant in this design, it comes out of the data.
input.why_general:
**Nothing in the pipeline names a domain. The test: run the derivation on two queries that share no vocabulary, no source type, and no rubric with task 72.**

---
### QUERY A — CLINICAL: "CAR-T vs bispecific antibodies in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma"

1. **RUBRIC — DERIVED.** The judge's own generator (`prompt/criteria_prompt_en.py`), handed only this text, returns weights and criteria. It will almost certainly produce criteria like "Comparative efficacy across trial endpoints", "Toxicity and safety profile (CRS/ICANS)", "Patient-selection and sequencing considerations", "Currency of evidence (2023+ trial readouts)". **We know this generator generalizes because we can prove it offline on all 100 tasks against the cached answer key (Part 3).** Weights will differ from task 72's — likely insight lower, comprehensiveness and instruction-following higher for a comparative-efficacy question. *Derived.*
2. **SCOPE GATE — DERIVED.** Source class extracted from the prompt: peer-reviewed clinical literature **plus registries plus conference abstracts (ASH/ASCO)** — because in myeloma the newest pivotal readouts land at conferences before journals. The prompt does not say "journal articles only", so unlike task 72 the gate **does not** exclude them. Recency window: aggressive (2022+), because the prompt names two therapy classes whose evidence base is <5 years old. *Derived. On task 72, the SAME code derives "journals only, English" because the prompt SAYS SO.*
3. **ANCHORS — DERIVED.** First search → highest-authority on-topic works within scope → KarMMa/CARTITUDE and MajesTEC/MonumenTAL trial papers emerge as most-cited, then citation-graph expansion (which is what reaches the mechanistic and sequencing literature keyword search misses — the exact reason it was essential for finding Autor-Levy-Murnane). *Derived. Zero hand-named papers.*
4. **MATRIX — DERIVED.** Rows = the generated criteria. Columns = the entity axis lifted from the criterion text: here **{CAR-T, bispecifics} × {ORR, PFS, OS, CRS grade≥3, ICANS, manufacturing/vein-to-vein time, prior-line eligibility}**. On task 72 the same extractor reads "across a diverse range of relevant industries" and produces industries. *The column axis comes out of the criterion string, not an engineer.*
5. **COMPOSITION.** The four sentence classes are domain-free: REPORT (a trial's ORR, extractively grounded), COMPARE (two trials' ORRs), SCOPE-DIFFERENCE (**"these two trials enrolled different prior-line populations"** — a fact about the cards, never a causal explanation), ANALYTICAL SYNTHESIS (**"If [CARTITUDE's 98% ORR in ≤3 prior lines, cited] and [MajesTEC's 63% in triple-refractory, cited], then the apparent CAR-T advantage may partly reflect line-of-therapy selection rather than intrinsic potency"** — hedged, tagged, ≥2 premise_ids, so the no-new-number and no-new-entity gates still run). Gap claims corpus-scoped: **"Within the 84 studies retrieved for this review, none reports a head-to-head randomized comparison."** *That is true by construction and it is exactly the sentence a hematologist would want.*
6. **MEASUREMENT.** Identical: ablate, ledger, panel, kill rules. (t75/t76/t78 on the panel are literally clinical questions.)
**HARDCODED: nothing.**

---
### QUERY B — HISTORICAL: "What caused the 2008 financial crisis?"

1. **RUBRIC — DERIVED.** Generator returns something like "Coverage of competing causal accounts (housing bubble / securitization / regulatory capture / global imbalances / monetary policy)", "Treatment of the historiographical debate", "Use of primary sources (FCIC report, Fed transcripts)", "Chronological clarity". Note readability and insight weights will look nothing like task 72's.
2. **SCOPE GATE — DERIVED.** Source class: **economic history journals + official inquiries (FCIC, Levin-Coburn) + central-bank archives + FRED/BIS data + serious monographs.** Recency window: **NONE — or rather, the derived window is "any", because a 2008 causal question has no recency requirement.** This is the case that proves the gate is derived: the SAME code that demands 2024+ for AI-and-labour demands nothing here. Sol's "recency-bonus for ≥2023" hardcoded weight would actively *damage* this query by down-ranking the 2010-2011 FCIC-era scholarship that is the canonical evidence.
3. **ANCHORS — DERIVED.** First search → most-authoritative on-topic works → Mian & Sufi, Gorton, Rajan, Bernanke's global-savings-glut speech, the FCIC report. **Citation-graph expansion is what reaches Minsky (1977) — whose title contains neither "2008" nor "financial crisis" and which no keyword search will ever return, yet which is the field's organizing framework.** This is the *identical* structural argument as Autor-Levy-Murnane, and it is why the graph mechanism is general rather than econ-specific.
4. **MATRIX — DERIVED.** Rows = generated criteria. Columns = the causal-account axis extracted from the criterion text: {housing bubble, securitization/CDO, ratings agencies, shadow-bank runs, regulatory capture, global imbalances, monetary policy} × {evidence type, principal proponents, principal critiques, quantitative magnitude}. **A cell filled means ≥2 independent sources establish it — same rule, different furniture.**
5. **COMPOSITION.** The classes carry straight over. ADJUDICATION — cellcog's highest-value move — is *native* here: "Mian and Sufi's household-leverage evidence and Gorton's repo-run evidence are not rival explanations but observations at different levels of the system — the household balance sheet and the wholesale funding market — and the choice of level is a substantive analytical commitment, not an innocent one." *That is precisely cellcog's level-of-analysis master key, reproduced from a `level` field on the cards, with no domain knowledge in the code.*
6. **THE FAITHFULNESS LINE IS THE SAME.** "We propose, as analytical synthesis, that…" is legal, tagged, hedged, and carries premise_ids. "Bernanke argued the crisis was caused by X" when he did not — **fraud, and impossible, in either domain.**
**HARDCODED: nothing.**

---
### WHAT IS DERIVED vs HARDCODED — the full ledger
**DERIVED AT RUNTIME:** rubric + dimension weights (from the judge's own generator); source class; recency window; language; topic gate; anchors; coverage-matrix rows AND columns; outline; stopping condition; effort allocation; report length target; the pass/fail gate itself.
**HARDCODED (and domain-free by inspection):** the four sentence classes; the extractive-span requirement; the mechanism pool = sentence's own cited cards; the corpus-scoped gap form; the ledger patch; the k=5 protocol; the kill rules; the ablation method.
**HARDCODED TO A DOMAIN: NOTHING.** And the falsifier is cheap and pre-registered: **if any constant in the system cannot be re-derived for t66 (Obsidian plugins) and t95 (Diamond Sutra) — two panel tasks with literally no academic literature — it is a hardcode and it comes out.** That is why those two tasks are ON the panel and not a topic-matched set of eight labour-economics questions.
input.faithfulness_position:
**THE LINE: no sentence may put words in a source's mouth. That is the whole rule, and it is not negotiable.**

**WHAT WE KEEP — because it is what makes this a research product people TRUST:**
1. **Every number, entity, finding, and attribution in the shipped artifact traces to an EXTRACTIVE SPAN of a card the sentence itself cites.** Fable is right that no LLM-written field may ever serve as the ground-truth span; the model may SELECT offsets, never compose them. A required output field with an absent input is the canonical invention condition.
2. **Fable's three refinements, kept verbatim** — they beat the operator's first formulation and I am not touching them: (a) the mechanism pool is the union of the **sentence's OWN cited cards**, never the corpus (a corpus-wide pool degenerates gate #6 at `synthesis_contract.py:197-205` into "does the sentence contain a word the field uses" and **flips our own ATTACK #1 from REJECT to PASS**); (b) **hedge + tag alone is COSTUME, never a license** — an analytical synthesis must be a CONDITIONAL over ≥2 admitted premises carrying `premise_ids`, so the no-new-number and no-new-entity gates STILL RUN; (c) gap claims are **corpus-scoped** — "Within the 137 journal articles retrieved for this review, none measures X" is true by construction and carries the identical scoring payoff as cellcog's false universal.
3. **The gates on the critical path.** `validate()` is imported at `cellcog_composer.py:49` and called NOWHERE; the only filter is a regex `_clean()` at `:342`. `cellcog_composer.py:167` copies `mechanisms` straight from LLM output while `claim`/`span` beside it ARE checked — a two-hop laundering channel. The hardcoded abstract at `:400-414` asserts a compliance claim regardless of corpus — fabrication with a score motive. **All three die, with a CI test proving a poisoned card produces zero poisoned sentences on the page.** This repo has shipped that class of bug three times (`225b323`, `b3acb72`, `947d2b5`); the fourth time it gets an assertion.

**WHAT WE DROP:**
1. **The span-entailment-of-every-sentence rule** (`entailment_judge.py:588`'s NEUTRAL clause: delete any sentence introducing a fact/mechanism "not present in THE SPAN"). It is why 98% of our sentences are bare facts on a task where the two reasoning criteria are worth **16% of the score**. It is replaced by the four typed classes, **not by nothing.**
2. **NEUTRAL-as-router.** A NEUTRAL verdict is the hallucination signal, not a green light to re-label. Rewrite-and-re-judge (max 2), else DELETE.
3. **Unscoped negative existentials.** Deterministic regex, zero hits.

**THE HONEST PART — AND IT IS THE POINT OF ARM A6.**
The fabrication probe will, I predict, show that **the judge cannot detect fabrication.** It reads cleaned prose with no citation validation whatsoever — `citation_accuracy` and `effective_citations` are "-" for all ten board systems. **If A6 comes back at Δ≈0, then faithfulness costs us score, and we keep it anyway.**

That is not a slogan; it is the actual decision, and I want it made with the number in hand rather than in the dark. Here is why the answer is still "keep it":
- **A benchmark number obtained by lying is the one result we could never publish, never demo, and never defend.** It is a liability disguised as an asset. A 0.60 that fabricates is a 0.00 — both plans say this and both are right.
- **The trust moat is not the score; it is that every claim in the report can be traced to a span in a real source.** That is a property no system on that leaderboard has, and it is the only thing here that is worth more than the leaderboard.
- **And the number itself is an asset.** "Our system scores X with full provenance; the same system without the honesty gates scores X+δ" is a *result*. It is the first honest measurement of what integrity costs in an LLM-judged research benchmark, and nobody has it.

**PROTECTS TRUST vs MERELY COSTS SCORE — the clean split:**
- *Protects trust:* the no-words-in-mouth rule; extractive-span provenance for every number/entity/attribution; premise_ids on every synthesis; corpus-scoped gap claims; the poisoned-card CI test; validate() on the critical path.
- *Merely costs score, and goes:* the demand that every sentence be entailed by a span; NEUTRAL-as-delete-everything; the ban on the reviewer's own marked reasoning.
We were enforcing the second list and calling it the first.

**I decline to give a point estimate for the final score, and that refusal IS the contribution.** Sol says 0.563. Fable says 0.572. Both numbers are the product of an unmeasured visibility-to-value conversion rate multiplied by lever deltas that nobody has ever observed. **WHAT I CAN PRICE, WITH CONFIDENCE, BECAUSE I VERIFIED THE COSTS:**
- **The instrument: ~$10 and 2 days.** P1 ledger patch (~6 lines at `deepresearch_bench_race.py:187`), P2 board calibration (10 systems × k=5 = $5.55), Part-2 ablation (7 arms × k=5 = $3.85), swap test ($0.55).
- **What that $10 returns:** (1) cellcog's REAL task-72 score, SE≈0.003 — the number both plans argue about and neither measured; (2) whether our gpt-5.5 judge IS the leaderboard's judge — an assumption every number in this project rests on and nobody has tested; (3) a MEASURED value-per-lever table (attribution / synthesis / recency / structure / scale), which sets the build order from data instead of arithmetic; (4) whether the judge can detect fabrication at all — the number that makes the faithfulness decision honest instead of pious; (5) R, the reference's raw weighted score, which is the denominator of every projection anyone has made.
- **The panel: $2.60 per full 8-task k=3 score.** Scoring is free. Composing is the entire cost. Both plans budget as if the reverse were true, and it distorts every design choice they make.

**THE REAL EXPECTED GAIN IS NOT A SCORE DELTA. It is the elimination of this project's dominant failure mode** — five commits (`225b323`, `b3acb72`, `fb228dd`, `e87f52e`, `947d2b5`) of levers that moved a metric while the mechanism never fired, or fired and bought nothing. Every gate in this design tests the mechanism before it reads the score, and the ablation prices the mechanism before we build it.

**IF FORCED TO STATE A PRIOR** (and I want it on the record so it can be falsified in 48 hours): the ablation will rank **A5 (scale) > A2 (synthesis) > A4 (structure) >> A1 (attribution) ≈ A3 (recency)**, because scale and synthesis are the only levers that move all 25 criteria at once and the instrument (V8) demands exactly that. If that ranking holds, **the corpus/retrieval program — the majority of both plans' engineering — is worth roughly +0.014 and should be built LAST, not first.** Honest reachable range on task 72: **0.52–0.58**, mode near wherever cellcog actually lands. Panel mean starts near 0.30, and dragging it to 0.50 is the actual work. If A6 shows the judge cannot see fabrication, our honest system will score **below** what a dishonest one would — and we ship the honest one, and we publish the number.
honest_risks: THE ABLATIONS MAY NOT BE CLEAN. Stripping attribution (A1) also shortens sentences and may perturb readability, contaminating the delta. Mitigation: length-neutral rewrites; a per-arm mechanism counter measured POST-cleaner (A1 must show in-prose author names 173 -> ~0 with word count moving <3%); and P1's per-dimension raw scores let us SEE contamination — if A1 moves readability, the rewrite was not clean and the arm is rerun. This is the single biggest threat to the whole design and I am not certain I can fully control it.

THE GRADIENT AT cellcog's 0.55 MAY NOT BE THE GRADIENT AT OUR 0.26. A feature worth +0.04 to a 16,334-word masterpiece may be worth 0 to a 2,183-word draft — feature value may interact with baseline quality. Partial mitigation: also run the top-ranked lever as an ADD to our own artifact and require the sign to agree (2 extra arms, $1.10). But if the interaction is strong, the ablation table is a biased prior and I would be building on it anyway.

OUR JUDGE MAY NOT BE THE LEADERBOARD'S JUDGE. We use openai/gpt-5.5 via OpenRouter; the board is 'gpt55' but we have never verified snapshot, temperature, or prompt-version parity. K0 catches it — but if K0 FIRES we have no route to the official judge, every board number (cellcog 0.5578, bodhi 0.5441) becomes soft, and we would be reduced to relative comparisons on our own instrument. That is a genuine dead end and I have no mitigation for it.

cellcog's TASK-72 ARTIFACT MAY BE ATYPICAL OF cellcog. Its 0.5578 is a 100-task mean; per-task deltas from corpus-wide range -0.0082 to +0.0194 across the board. Measuring it directly could show cellcog's task 72 sits BELOW bodhi's 0.5441 — in which case both plans have spent their entire ceiling analysis chasing a phantom, and the real bar was bodhi's measured number all along. (This is a risk to the PLANS, not to my design — my design measures it. But it means the answer may embarrass everyone.)

THE GENERALITY PANEL MAKES THE WHEEL 8x SLOWER TO TURN, because composing is the real cost and every turn now needs 8 composes rather than 1. Mitigation: full 8-task panel at turn 0 and at every kill-rule checkpoint; a 3-task sub-panel (one academic, one non-academic, one clinical) between checkpoints. This is a real, unavoidable tax and it will make people want to skip it — which is exactly how we end up shipping the 0.26 system.

THE OFFLINE RUBRIC-RECONSTRUCTION PROOF IS A MECHANISM TEST, NOT AN OUTCOME TEST. Perfectly reconstructing the judge's 25 criteria on 100/100 tasks proves our DERIVATION is faithful; it says nothing about whether the report we then write is good. I am claiming it as cheap evidence of generality, not as evidence of quality, and it would be easy to over-sell.

50 OF THE 100 BENCHMARK TASKS ARE CHINESE. cellcog's 0.5578 is a corpus-wide mean including all 50. We will not beat it corpus-wide without composing in Chinese, and I am not proposing that. Every claim here must be scoped to per-task and to the EN panel, or it is a units error of exactly the kind Fable already caught once in this project.

IF A6 SHOWS THE JUDGE CANNOT DETECT FABRICATION, we will have measured, precisely, what our integrity costs us in score — and someone will be tempted to spend it. The kill rule (K5: one fabricated claim on a shipped page burns the artifact) has to hold under that temptation, at 3am, when we are 0.008 short of the gate. That is a governance risk, not a technical one, and it is the one I trust least.

THE ABLATION MEASURES DESTRUCTION, NOT CONSTRUCTION. Knowing that removing cellcog's synthesis section costs it 0.03 does not tell us we can BUILD a synthesis section worth 0.03 — cellcog's is written by a frontier system with taste. The ablation gives an upper bound on lever value and a ranking; it does not give an achievability estimate, and I will not pretend it does.
